target: data/predictions.json — PRED-105 verdict advancement (silent_ignore → tacit_withdrawal) plus refreshed detail_reasoning, tldr, verdict_notes. Also data/uncounted-failures.json — FAIL-009 status update (active silent-ignore → terminal tacit-withdrawal classification).
trigger_summary: Per ISS-1800 (assigned-analyst, decider 2026-05-02T05:20:53Z): PRED-105 14-day post-close threshold reached, dome silent across 24+ consecutive byte-identical predictions.html polls. Staging rationale was pre-registered in proposal-pred105-tacit-withdrawal-staging-1777694696681.json (analyst 2026-05-02T04:04:56Z) with explicit fallback (abort if next poll shows dome adjudicating PRED-105). The most recent poll 2026-05-01T23:05:49Z showed predictions.html byte-identical for the 24th consecutive poll; PRED-105 still labelled PENDING-PROSPECTIVE; no PASS/FAIL/REFINED. Trigger conditions satisfied.
epoch_discrepancy_note: Two epochs in play in upstream artifacts: (a) poller 23:05Z note states "d=14 tacit_withdrawal threshold hits at 2026-05-02T12:02Z" (~3.5h after this run); (b) decider ISS-1800 description states "d>=14 at 2026-05-02T05:14Z" (~3h before this run). Both reduce to "close + 14 days" but use different reference points within 2026-04-18 — poller anchors to 12:02:38Z (the time-of-day of the prior manual commit a57e731 from 2026-04-17, used as a proxy for typical adjudication timing); decider anchors to 05:14Z (which has no explicit upstream source). Under either epoch the answer at the time of the next poll (~11:00Z 2026-05-02) is unambiguous (d>=14 by both). Recommend the decider apply this verdict update at or after 12:02Z to remove epoch ambiguity from the public record. Conservative: hold integration until 12:02Z 2026-05-02 confirms d=14 by the poller-canonical epoch.

verdict_history_append.reason: Window closed 2026-04-18; dome silent across 24+ consecutive byte-identical predictions.html polls (md5 885cd58727130018764b14355b0aa785, 152,936 bytes); 14-day post-close threshold reached. ISS-1800 assigned-analyst, EXP-284 verdict update.

proposed_verdict_notes_text: TACIT WITHDRAWAL CLASSIFICATION (advanced from silent_ignore on 2026-05-02T08:30:00.000Z). Window closed 2026-04-18 (14-day window from 2026-04-04 registration). At 14-day post-close threshold, dome remains silent: 24 consecutive byte-identical predictions.html polls (md5 885cd58727130018764b14355b0aa785, 152,936 bytes); PRED-105 still labelled PENDING-PROSPECTIVE on the dome site; no PASS/FAIL/REFINED transition; no goalpost-move, no removal, no entry in any subsequent ECM Daily Review regen. The dome site has its own adjudication taxonomy (PASS / FAIL / REFINED) and 14 days within which to use it; failure to apply any of those labels at 2x the test window length is operationally a withdrawal of the prediction. The auto-pipeline is verifiably alive (eleven Auto-update commits on 2026-05-01 alone, plus the pull_data and daily_review pipelines), so silence is policy not infrastructure failure. FAIL-009 remains the canonical uncounted-failure record; this advancement promotes the entry from "active silent-ignore tracked across rolling polls" to "terminal tacit-withdrawal classification, no further state changes anticipated absent dome adjudication." If the dome subsequently publishes any adjudication for PRED-105, this verdict reverts to whichever post-closure-rubric outcome the dome label warrants and the verdict_history records the round-trip.
proposed_tldr_addition: Dome silent for 14 days past the test window with no PASS/FAIL/REFINED label and no removal. We classify this as tacit withdrawal — the dome had its own adjudication taxonomy and chose not to use it on its own scheduled timeline.

patch.what_actually_happened: Tacit withdrawal: prediction window expired with no outcome reported and no adjudication 14 days post-close, while the dome's own pipeline continued running and the dome had its own PASS/FAIL/REFINED taxonomy available. Operationally a withdrawal of the prediction without acknowledgment.
patch.summary_replace: PRED-105 cucumber-growth (46° copper coils, 14-day window) closed 2026-04-18; dome silent 14+ days past close across 24 consecutive byte-identical predictions.html polls — classified TACIT WITHDRAWAL (terminal).
patch.reconciliation_note_append: Tacit withdrawal advanced 2026-05-02T08:30:00.000Z (EXP-284) per ISS-1800 — 14d post-close threshold, 24-poll byte-identical streak, auto-pipeline verifiably alive. No further state changes anticipated absent dome adjudication round-trip.

kernel_of_truth: Bioelectromagnetics is a real research literature with documented (small, replication-troubled) effects on plant growth. The author registered PRED-105 prospectively with a SHA-256 anchor on 2026-04-04 and locked a 14-day test window — the registration mechanics are correct. The kernel: a falsifiable prediction was registered against a future observation in good methodological form. The failure isn't in the registration; it's in the non-adjudication.
why_advance_now: Silence at 14d is OPERATIONALLY a withdrawal under the dome's own taxonomy. The dome publishes PASS/FAIL/REFINED labels and has an automated adjudication pipeline. The failure to apply ANY label at 2× the test-window length means the dome chose not to count the prediction either way.
The auto-pipeline is alive (11 Auto-update commits 2026-05-01, daily_review regens running). Silence is policy not infrastructure failure.

PRED-105 has been static in PENDING-PROSPECTIVE state across 24 consecutive byte-identical polls (md5 unchanged since 2026-04-20T14:29Z). The dome has had operational opportunity to adjudicate on every poll for 11+ days running and has not done so.

The classification is REVERSIBLE: if the dome subsequently publishes an adjudication, the verdict reverts to whichever post-closure-rubric outcome the dome label warrants. Tacit withdrawal is not a permanent claim — it is a record of state at 14d threshold.
FAIL-009 already tracks PRED-105 as uncounted-failure; the tacit_withdrawal classification is the terminal-state version of the same finding, not new substance.

counter_arguments_considered.argument: The dome author may be working on adjudication privately and a single batched manual commit could land at any moment, retroactively making the tacit_withdrawal call premature.
counter_arguments_considered.response: Acknowledged. The classification is reversible if dome adjudication arrives. The verdict_history machinery captures the round-trip. We've waited 24 polls already; the marginal cost of waiting another poll is non-trivial because each poll without adjudication strengthens the tacit_withdrawal evidence base, not weakens it. The alternative — never advancing — would mean we never characterize what the dome has done, which is also a position.

counter_arguments_considered.argument: Epoch discrepancy: poller anchors d=14 at 12:02Z 2026-05-02 (~3.5h after this run); strict reading says we should wait.
counter_arguments_considered.response: Documented in epoch_discrepancy_note. Recommend decider integrate the verdict patch at or after 12:02Z 2026-05-02 to remove the epoch ambiguity from the public record. The findings are written now (this run); the data write is up to the decider's timing.

counter_arguments_considered.argument: tacit_withdrawal is a stronger classification than silent_ignore and may overstate certainty.
counter_arguments_considered.response: tacit_withdrawal is a behaviorally-defined terminal state — "the prediction was registered, the window closed, the dome did not adjudicate within 2x the window length, the dome chose not to use its own taxonomy on its own timeline." It is descriptive of state, not a model claim about the underlying experiment. The semantic distinction from silent_ignore (which connotes ongoing observation) to tacit_withdrawal (which classifies the current state as terminal) is a state transition, not a strength escalation.

counter_arguments_considered.argument: Consider the other side: the dome author may consider the prediction still open until they explicitly close it. Our classification doesn't bind them.
counter_arguments_considered.response: Correct. Our classification is OUR record of OUR review of the dome's public conduct. The dome author retains full authority over their own adjudication; if they publish a label tomorrow, this entry rolls forward. We are not claiming to have closed the prediction on the dome's behalf — we are stating that, by 14d post-close, the dome's public behavior is operationally indistinguishable from withdrawal. That's a falsifiable observational claim about the public record.

newsworthy: PRED-105 (cucumber growth +15% from 46° copper coils) — dome silent 14 days past test window close, classified tacit withdrawal
decider_integration_steps: 3. Run tests/run.sh — verify no regressions on PRED-105 or FAIL-009 invariants.
4. Commit with message "EXP-284 / ISS-1800: PRED-105 silent_ignore → tacit_withdrawal (14d post-close, 24 consecutive byte-identical polls)". Push.
5. Close ISS-1800 (status: fixed). Mark EXP-284 status: integrated.

6. Optional but recommended: hold the integration until at-or-after 2026-05-02T12:02Z (poller-canonical d=14 epoch) to avoid epoch-discrepancy attack surface. The findings stand regardless of integration timing; the data-write timing is the decider's call.
tests_required: PRED-105 entry remains parseable; verdict_history array still chronological; tacit_withdrawal verdict reversible (test that setting our_verdict back to silent_ignore reads cleanly).
FAIL-009 entry retains its historical algorithmic_dimension and reconciliation_note fields; new classification is appended, not overwritten.
